title: The first thing I do at work
summary: Describe the very first action you do when you arrive at your workplace.
topic: The first thing I do at work
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about the first thing you do at work. Say where you work, what time you arrive, what your first action is, and why you do it. Use present simple.
hintStarters: I work in a small office in Seoul and every morning…
When I sit down, the first thing I do is…
sampleText: I work in a small office in Seoul. I arrive at my desk at eight thirty every morning. The other people in the office often check their email first. I do something different. The first thing I do is fill the small kettle in the kitchen. I boil water for tea. I do not start my computer yet. I take a green ceramic cup from my drawer. I put one bag of green tea in the cup. I pour the hot water slowly. I watch the colour change for one minute. Then I take the cup back to my desk and I sit down. Only then I open my computer. I take a small piece of paper. I write three things I want to finish today. I do not write more than three. After that, I start my email. This small ritual takes only seven minutes. But it makes me calm before a busy day. I feel ready when I start. A simple cup of tea is a quiet door into work.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một văn phòng nhỏ ở Seoul
một cái cốc sứ màu xanh

sampleAnnotations.phrase: three things I want to finish
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: ba việc tôi muốn hoàn thành
cánh cửa thầm lặng vào công việc

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'The first thing I do is …'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Cấu trúc 'The first thing I do is + động từ nguyên mẫu' để nhấn mạnh hành động đầu tiên: 'The first thing I do is fill the kettle'. Không thêm 'to' giữa 'is' và động từ.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: The structure 'The first thing I do is + base verb' emphasises the first action: 'The first thing I do is fill the kettle'. Do not add 'to' between 'is' and the verb.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Linkers 'before' and 'after that'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'before a busy day' giới thiệu sự việc xảy ra trước; 'after that' nối câu chuyện sang phần tiếp theo. Dùng 'before + danh từ/V-ing', 'after that' đứng đầu câu mới.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'before a busy day' marks something earlier; 'after that' moves the story forward. Use 'before + noun/-ing'; 'after that' usually starts a new sentence.

sampleTranslationVi: Tôi làm việc trong một văn phòng nhỏ ở Seoul. Tôi đến bàn làm việc lúc tám giờ ba mươi mỗi sáng. Những người khác trong văn phòng thường kiểm tra email trước. Tôi làm điều gì đó khác. Việc đầu tiên tôi làm là đổ đầy ấm nhỏ trong bếp. Tôi đun nước pha trà. Tôi chưa khởi động máy tính. Tôi lấy một cái cốc sứ màu xanh từ ngăn kéo. Tôi cho một túi trà xanh vào cốc. Tôi rót nước nóng từ từ. Tôi nhìn nước đổi màu trong một phút. Rồi tôi mang cốc về bàn và ngồi xuống. Chỉ lúc đó tôi mới mở máy tính. Tôi lấy một mẩu giấy nhỏ. Tôi viết ba việc tôi muốn hoàn thành hôm nay. Tôi không viết hơn ba việc. Sau đó, tôi mở email. Nghi thức nhỏ này chỉ mất bảy phút. Nhưng nó làm tôi bình tĩnh trước một ngày bận. Tôi cảm thấy sẵn sàng khi bắt đầu. Một tách trà đơn giản là cánh cửa thầm lặng vào công việc.

options: In a small office in Seoul
In a big factory in Tokyo
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I work in a small office in Seoul'.
The text says 'I arrive at my desk at eight thirty every morning'.

mcQuestions.prompt: What do most other people in the office do first?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'The other people in the office often check their email first'.

mcQuestions.prompt: What is the writer's first action?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'The first thing I do is fill the small kettle in the kitchen'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for 'The first thing I do'.

mcQuestions.prompt: What kind of cup does he use?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I take a green ceramic cup from my drawer'.

mcQuestions.prompt: How many tasks does he write?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I write three things I want to finish today. I do not write more than three'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the number of tasks.

options: The first thing I do is fill the kettle.
The first thing I do is to fill the kettle.
The first thing I do fill the kettle.
First thing the kettle I fill.
mcQuestions.explanation: Use 'is + base verb' without 'to': 'is fill the kettle'.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'is' in this pattern, do not add 'to'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence in present simple.
options: I am pouring the hot water every morning.
I poured the hot water every morning.
I pour the hot water slowly.
I will pour the hot water now.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present simple describes the regular action: 'I pour the hot water slowly'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the form 'I pour' for a regular action.

mcQuestions.prompt: How long does the ritual take?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'This small ritual takes only seven minutes'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the time near the end.

mcQuestions.prompt: How does the writer feel after the ritual?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'it makes me calm before a busy day. I feel ready when I start'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the feeling words near the end.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why does a small morning ritual at work often make us more productive than starting straight away?
